title: My AI bot burned through my API budget overnight. So I built an open-source tool to make sure it never happens again.
summary: An autonomous AI news engine unexpectedly spent $4 in a day when a container restart cleared the in-memory budget tracker. This prompted the author to build toad-eye, an open-source observability toolkit for LLM systems that asserts on quality behavior beyond just logging requests.
content:
I run an autonomous AI news engine called El Sapo Cripto. It monitors 25+ RSS feeds, scores articles, generates Spanish-language summaries with Gemini, creates images, and publishes to Telegram and X. All day, every day, zero human intervention.
One morning I woke up to a ~$4 bill from Google. Not a lot, right? But my usual daily spend was under $0.25. Something was very wrong.

My app runs on Railway. Railway occasionally restarts containers. My budget tracker lived in memory. Restart = budget reset = the bot thought it had a fresh $0 balance and went wild. Gemini Flash calls piled up - summarizing, re-summarizing, processing articles it had already processed.
I caught it by accident, scrolling through the billing page. There was no alert. No dashboard. No way to see what happened without manually reading through logs.
And here's the thing that really bothered me: the app was returning 200 OK on every request. Prometheus would've shown zero errors. Traditional monitoring would've said "everything's fine" while the bot was eating money.
I started looking around for tools to monitor LLM calls. Found a few options: Langfuse, Helicone, OpenLLMetry. All solid projects. But they all shared the same limitation — they show you what your LLM is doing, but they don't tell you if it's doing it well.
I don't just need to see that my bot made 200 Gemini calls. I need to know: did the summaries get worse after I changed the prompt last Tuesday? Are error rates creeping up because the provider is having issues? Is the cost per article going up because responses are getting longer? Is the model quietly refusing to summarize certain topics?
I'm a Senior SDET by background. 8+ years building test frameworks and quality infrastructure. In the testing world, we don't just log requests — we assert on behavior, detect regressions, set quality gates. None of the existing LLM tools did that.
So I built one.

toad-eye is an open-source observability toolkit for LLM systems, built on OpenTe

zh.title: 我的AI机器人一夜之间烧光了我的API预算。所以我建立了一个开源工具来确保这种情况永远不会再发生。
zh.summary: 当容器重启清除了内存中的预算追踪器时，一个自主AI新闻引擎意外地在一天内花费了4美元。这促使作者构建了toad-eye，一个用于LLM系统的开源可观测性工具包，它超越了仅仅记录请求的质量行为断言。

fr.title: Mon bot IA a épuisé mon budget API en une nuit. J'ai donc créé un outil open-source pour m'assurer que cela ne se reproduise jamais.
fr.summary: Un moteur de nouvelles IA autonome a dépensé 4 dollars de manière inattendue en une journée lorsqu'un redémarrage de conteneur a effacé le suivi du budget en mémoire. Cela a incité l'auteur à créer toad-eye, une boîte à outils d'observabilité open-source pour les systèmes LLM qui affirme sur les comportements de qualité au-delà du simple enregistrement des demandes.

de.title: Mein KI-Bot hat mein API-Budget über Nacht aufgebraucht. Also baute ich ein Open-Source-Tool, um sicherzustellen, dass dies nie wieder passiert.
de.summary: Ein autonomes KI-Nachrichtenmodul gab unerwartet 4 Dollar in einem Tag aus, als ein Container-Neustart den speicherinternen Budget-Tracker löschte. Dies veranlasste den Autor, toad-eye zu entwickeln, ein Open-Source-Observability-Toolkit für LLM-Systeme, das die Qualitätsbehaviors jenseits des einfachen Loggings von Anfragen assertiert.

es.title: Mi bot de IA agotó mi presupuesto de API durante la noche. Así que construí una herramienta de código abierto para asegurar que nunca vuelva a suceder.
es.summary: Un motor de noticias IA autónomo gastó inesperadamente $4 en un día cuando un reinicio de contenedor borró el rastreador de presupuesto en memoria. Esto impulsó al autor a construir toad-eye, un kit de herramientas de observabilidad de código abierto para sistemas LLM que aserta en comportamientos de calidad más allá del simple registro de solicitudes.

title: Day 14 – When Not To Use Agentic AI
summary: Agentic AI introduces costs, fragility, and unpredictability when applied to deterministic problems. The post outlines scenarios where agents should be avoided—fixed rules, real-time systems, tight budgets, and safety-critical operations—recommending code and rules instead.
content:
The Most Important Agentic AI Lesson 🚫🤖


Agentic AI is powerful.
That’s exactly why it’s dangerous to overuse.
Some problems should not be solved with agents — not because agents are weak, but because they are the wrong abstraction.
Using agentic AI in the wrong place leads to:
higher costs

fragile systems

unpredictable behavior

loss of trust



Knowing when not to use agents is a mark of maturity.
If a problem is deterministic, repeatable, and well-defined — you probably don’t need an agent.
Agents shine when:
goals are fuzzy

paths are unknown

decisions require judgment



They struggle when:
rules are fixed

outcomes must be exact

failure tolerance is near zero







  
  
  1️⃣ When Rules Beat Reasoning



  
  
  🚫 Don’t Use Agents For



tax calculations

invoice generation

interest computation

data validation rules



These problems already have:
clear inputs

deterministic logic

provable correctness




  
  
  Better Choice ✅


Code + Tests + Monitoring
Adding an agent here only introduces variance.
Agents:
think

plan

reflect

call tools



All of this adds variable latency.
responses must be <100ms

real-time systems are involved

users expect instant feedback



Examples:
fraud checks in payment flows

real-time bidding

control systems




  
  
  Better Choice ✅


Rules + Models (no loops)
Agent costs scale with:
number of steps

tool calls

reflection loops




  
  
  🚫 Avoid Agents When



budgets are tight

cost overruns are unacceptable

usage spikes are unpredictable



Examples:
high-volume transactional systems

batch jobs with millions of rows




  
  
  Better Choice ✅


Batch pipelines + deterministic logic
Agents can:
misinterpret goals

call wrong tools

stop too early or too late




  
  
  🚫 Avoid Agents When



safety is critical

legal consequences exist

rollback is impossible



Examples:
medical dosage systems

financial transfers

security policy enforcement




  
  
  Better Choice ✅


Human-in-the-loop or hard-

zh.title: 第14天 – 何时不使用代理AI
zh.summary: 当应用于确定性问题时，代理AI引入了成本、脆弱性和不可预测性。这篇文章概述了应该避免使用代理的场景——固定规则、实时系统、紧张的预算和安全关键操作——建议使用代码和规则代替。

fr.title: Jour 14 – Quand ne pas utiliser l'IA agentic
fr.summary: L'IA agentic introduit des coûts, une fragilité et une imprévisibilité lorsqu'elle est appliquée à des problèmes déterministes. Le post décrit les scénarios où les agents doivent être évités—règles fixes, systèmes temps réel, budgets serrés et opérations critiques pour la sécurité—en recommandant plutôt du code et des règles.

de.title: Tag 14 – Wann man agentische KI nicht verwenden sollte
de.summary: Agentische KI führt Kosten, Fragilität und Unvorhersehbarkeit ein, wenn sie auf deterministische Probleme angewendet wird. Der Beitrag beschreibt Szenarien, in denen Agenten vermieden werden sollten – feste Regeln, Echtzeitsysteme, knappe Budgets und sicherheitskritische Operationen – und empfiehlt stattdessen Code und Regeln.

es.title: Día 14 – Cuándo no usar IA de agentes
es.summary: La IA agentic introduce costos, fragilidad e impredictibilidad cuando se aplica a problemas deterministas. La publicación describe escenarios donde se deben evitar los agentes—reglas fijas, sistemas en tiempo real, presupuestos ajustados y operaciones críticas para la seguridad—recomendando código y reglas en su lugar.

title: I Built a Personalized AI Life Coach with Obsidian + Claude — Here's the Exact Setup
summary: Combining Obsidian as a personal knowledge base with Claude creates an AI that retains user context across conversations instead of starting from scratch. The system organizes notes into folders for periodic reflections, projects, and learning to ground every interaction in persistent knowledge.
content:
Most people use AI the same way they use Google: ask a question, get an answer, close the tab. The conversation resets every time. The AI never learns who you are.
I wanted something different. I wanted an AI that knows my goals, my history, my patterns, my blind spots — and actually uses that context to give me advice that fits me, not a generic user.
After a few months of iteration, I built a system that does exactly that. It combines Obsidian as a personal knowledge base with Claude as the reasoning layer. Here's how it works and how you can replicate it.
The limitation of most AI interactions isn't intelligence — it's memory. You spend the first five minutes of every conversation explaining who you are, what you're working on, and what you've already tried. By the time you get to the actual question, you've burned your patience and the AI's context window.
The fix is to stop starting from scratch. If the AI has persistent access to a structured set of notes about your life, goals, and work, every conversation begins from a foundation of context. You ask the question; it already knows the backstory.
Obsidian is the right tool for this because it stores everything as plain Markdown files, which means an AI can read them directly. No export step, no special integration, no API key. Just files.
The vault has a simple folder structure:
10_Periodic Notes/     → Daily and weekly notes
20_Areas/              → Health, finances, relationships, career
30_Projects/           → Active projects with their own notes
40_Career/             → Resume info, target companies, interview prep
50_Learning/           → Study notes, book summaries, courses
60_Reference/          → Guides, gear lists, setup docs, evergreen reference

Every day I write a brief daily note using a consistent template: mood, energy, what I'm working on, habits, what I learned, what I'd do differently. Over time these accumulate into a detailed picture of how I actually operate — not how I imagine I operate.

zh.title: 我用Obsidian + Claude建立了一个个性化AI生活教练 — 以下是确切的设置
zh.summary: 将Obsidian作为个人知识库与Claude结合，创建了一个AI，在对话中保留用户上下文，而不是从头开始。系统将笔记组织到文件夹中，用于定期反思、项目和学习，将每次交互都基于持久的知识。

Le système organise les notes en dossiers pour les réflexions périodiques, les projets et l'apprentissage pour fonder chaque interaction dans la connaissance persistante.

title: My Keploy Contribution: Resource Management in Go and Open Source Journey
summary: While contributing to Keploy, an open-source API testing tool, the author found and fixed use-after-close bugs where files were closed before operations completed. The fix applied Go's defer pattern to ensure proper resource cleanup regardless of function exit paths.

zh.title: 我的Keploy贡献：Go中的资源管理和开源之旅
zh.summary: 在为开源API测试工具Keploy做贡献时，作者发现并修复了使用后关闭的错误，其中文件在操作完成前被关闭。修复应用了Go的defer模式来确保无论函数退出路径如何都能正确清理资源。

fr.title: Ma Contribution à Keploy : Gestion des Ressources en Go et Voyage en Open Source
fr.summary: En contribuant à Keploy, un outil de test API open-source, l'auteur a trouvé et corrigé des bogues use-after-close où les fichiers étaient fermés avant la fin des opérations. La correction applique le pattern defer de Go pour assurer un nettoyage approprié des ressources indépendamment des chemins de sortie de la fonction.

de.title: Mein Keploy-Beitrag: Ressourcenverwaltung in Go und Open-Source-Reise
de.summary: Bei der Beitragserstellung zu Keploy, einem Open-Source-API-Test-Tool, fand und behob der Autor Use-After-Close-Fehler, bei denen Dateien vor Abschluss der Vorgänge geschlossen wurden. Die Korrektur wendet Gos defer-Pattern an, um ordnungsgemäße Ressourcenbereinigung unabhängig von den Funktionsausstiegspfaden sicherzustellen.

es.title: Mi Contribución a Keploy: Gestión de Recursos en Go y Viaje en Código Abierto
es.summary: Al contribuir a Keploy, una herramienta de prueba de API de código abierto, el autor encontró y corrigió errores de use-after-close donde los archivos se cerraban antes de que se completaran las operaciones. La solución aplicó el patrón defer de Go para garantizar la limpieza adecuada de recursos independientemente de las rutas de salida de la función.

title: The Only Honest Guide to Web Search in Ollama
summary: Most Ollama web search tutorials fail because they rely on DuckDuckGo's non-functional free API. Testing all approaches revealed SearXNG as the best option—self-hosted, private, free, with real results—and emphasized proper streaming and timeout handling.
content:
The Only Honest Guide to Web Search in Ollama


Every other tutorial gets this wrong. Here's what actually works.
I wanted to add web search to my local Ollama setup. I found a Medium article
It returned empty results. Every time. For every query.
So I spent two days testing every approach on a real Debian 13 server with
Every tutorial recommends this:
response = requests.get(
    "https://api.duckduckgo.com/",
    params={"q": query, "format": "json"}
)

Here's what it actually returns:
{
  "Abstract": "",
  "AbstractText": "",
  "RelatedTopics": [],
  "Results": []
}

Empty. I verified it with curl:
curl "https://api.duckduckgo.com/?q=ollama+latest+version&format=json&no_html=1"

The meta section reveals why — it's a test endpoint in "development" state,
DuckDuckGo's free API is not a real search API.
After testing every option, here's the honest comparison:



Backend
API Key
Real Results
Private
Cost




DuckDuckGo free API
❌
❌ Empty
✅
Free


SearXNG self-hosted
❌
✅
✅ Full
Free


Google Custom Search
✅
✅ Excellent
❌
100/day free


Bing Search API
✅
✅
❌
1000/month free


OpenClaw built-in
❌
✅
⚠️
Free



Winner: SearXNG. Self-hosted, private, no API key, real results from
The second mistake most tutorials make: no streaming.
# This will timeout on any real query
response = requests.post(OLLAMA_API, json=payload, timeout=60)

Ollama needs time to think, search, and generate. 60 seconds isn't enough.
with requests.post(OLLAMA_API, json=payload, stream=True) as response:
    for line in response.iter_lines():
        chunk = json.loads(line)
        content = chunk.get("message", {}).get("content", "")
        if content:
            print(content, end="", flush=True)

No timeout. Real-time output. Works every time.
Not all Ollama models support tool calling. Tested on Ollama 0.18.2:



Model
Tool Calling
Speed on CPU




llama3.1:8B
✅ Reliable
~2-5 min


qwen3:8B
✅ Excellent
⚠️ 16+ min*


mistral-nemo:12b
⚠️ Moderate
~3-7 min


deepseek-r1:14b
⚠️ Limited
Very slow

Tester toutes les approches a révélé que SearXNG était la meilleure option—auto-hébergée, privée, gratuite, avec des résultats réels—et a souligné l'importance du streaming approprié et de la gestion des délais d'expiration.

title: I Built a Tool So Claude Code Can Use My Colab GPU
summary: Claude-colab bridges Claude Code and Google Colab by providing direct GPU access through an encrypted Flask API and Cloudflare tunnel. Users can run GPU-intensive tasks seamlessly without manual copy-pasting between platforms.
content:
The Problem


I use Claude Code daily. It can read my files, run bash commands, edit code — but the moment I need GPU compute, I'm back to copy-pasting between my terminal and Google Colab like it's 2020.
I was working on a Videogen. The image generation runs locally on my Mac, but the model needs a GPU. Every time I wanted to test an image-to-video model, I had to:
Open Colab
Paste my code
Run cells manually
Copy results back
Repeat 50 times
So I built claude-colab — a bridge that gives Claude Code direct GPU access through Google Colab.
Colab (T4/A100 GPU)                     Your Machine
┌────────────────────┐                 ┌─────────────────┐
│ Flask API          │◄── HTTPS ──────►│ CLI / MCP Server│
│ E2E Encrypted      │  (cloudflared)  │                 │
│ Bearer token auth  │                 │ Claude Code     │
└────────────────────┘                 │ gains GPU tools │
                                       └─────────────────┘

Three layers:
Colab notebook — Flask API + Cloudflare tunnel on the GPU
CLI — pip install claude-colab, human-friendly commands
MCP server — Claude Code sees GPU tools natively
pip install claude-colab
# Open the notebook in Colab, run all cells, copy the connection string
claude-colab connect cc://TOKEN:KEY@your-tunnel.trycloudflare.com

That's it. Claude now has a GPU.
claude-colab status                    # GPU info
claude-colab exec "nvidia-smi"         # Run any command
claude-colab python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))"
claude-colab upload model.py /content/model.py
claude-colab download /content/results.csv ./results.csv

Or with the MCP server, Claude calls these as tools directly — no copy-paste, no browser tabs.
I didn't want my code traveling through Cloudflare in plaintext. Every request and response body is encrypted with Fernet (AES-128-CBC + HMAC-SHA256) before it hits the tunnel.



Who
Can see
Cannot see




Cloudflare
URL paths, timing
Your code, data, results


Google
Everything on the VM

title: I Gave Up Trying to Make Money From My Budget App. Here's What Happened Next.
summary: Monee is a permanently free budget app with no monetization plan, since browser storage costs nothing to host at any scale. Its usage pattern differs from traditional apps—users engage during impulsive spending moments rather than scheduled finance sessions.
content:
Three weeks ago I launched Monee -- a budget tracker with no accounts, no server, no subscription, and no business model.
That last one is the weird part.
Every indie hacker's story eventually gets to the monetization slide. Freemium, then $9/mo for the good stuff. Ads, once you have traffic. Enterprise tier, once you have credibility.
I couldn't do it. The whole point of Monee is that it's free because it's free -- not because you're in a trial. localStorage doesn't cost me money to host your data. Zero users or a million users, my bill is the same: $0.
So I made it free. Permanently. No plan to change that.
Traffic didn't explode. (I expected that.)
But here's what surprised me: the engagement pattern looked completely different from every app I've launched before.
Most apps: 80% of users bounce on day 1. The 20% who stick around eventually churn when they find something better.
Monee: people open it on their phones in moments of impulsive spending. Not during a Saturday "let me get my finances together" session. During a Tuesday lunch when they're about to order something they shouldn't.
That's a different use case than I designed for. And it changed how I think about what the app even is.
Here's the honest tension: I built something people find useful. I have no way to sustain it beyond "I enjoy building it and Vercel's free tier exists."
That's fine right now. But it raises a real question -- can a tool be genuinely free forever and still have a creator behind it who can keep improving it?
Some options I've considered:
GitHub Sponsors: people can optionally support. But almost nobody does for free tools.
"Pro" export features: CSV is free, but richer exports could be paid. Destroys the simplicity promise.
Adjacent paid tool: build something people will actually pay for, fund Monee as a side effect.
I haven't decided. But I'm watching.
Making something free-forever forces you to be ruthless about scope. Every feature I add is a feature I maintain for free, indef

zh.title: 我放弃了从我的预算应用赚钱的尝试。接下来发生了什么。
zh.summary: Monee是一个永久免费的预算应用，没有货币化计划，因为浏览器存储在任何规模上的托管成本都为零。它的使用模式与传统应用不同——用户在冲动消费时刻而不是在预定的财务会议期间进行互动。

fr.title: J'ai renoncé à essayer de monétiser mon application budgétaire. Voici ce qui s'est passé ensuite.
fr.summary: Monee est une application budgétaire gratuitement permanente sans plan de monétisation, car le stockage du navigateur ne coûte rien à héberger à n'importe quelle échelle. Son modèle d'utilisation diffère des applications traditionnelles : les utilisateurs s'engagent lors de moments de dépenses impulsifs plutôt que lors de sessions financières programmées.

de.title: Ich habe aufgegeben, mit meiner Budget-App Geld zu verdienen. Das ist danach passiert.
de.summary: Monee ist eine dauerhaft kostenlose Budget-App ohne Monetarisierungsplan, da die Browser-Speicherung auf jeder Ebene kostenlös zu hosten ist. Das Nutzungsmuster unterscheidet sich von traditionellen Apps – Benutzer interagieren in Momenten impulsiver Ausgaben statt in geplanten Finanzsitzungen.

es.title: Renuncié a intentar ganar dinero con mi aplicación presupuestaria. Esto es lo que pasó después.
es.summary: Monee es una aplicación presupuestaria permanentemente gratuita sin plan de monetización, ya que el almacenamiento del navegador no cuesta nada alojar a cualquier escala. Su patrón de uso difiere de las aplicaciones tradicionales: los usuarios se comprometen en momentos de gasto impulsivo en lugar de sesiones financieras programadas.

title: How I built a Next.js app with 1,500+ localized routes and perfect Technical SEO 🚀
summary: ProMetadata uses Next.js to serve 1,500+ statically generated routes across 20 languages, implementing dynamic middleware, hreflang tags, and automated sitemap generation. The architecture achieves multi-language SEO at scale while maintaining perfect Core Web Vitals.
content:
Building a micro-SaaS is only half the battle. Getting Google to care about it is the real challenge. 
When I set out to build ProMetadata—a suite of 14 tools to inject, extract, clean, and score image/PDF metadata—I knew my growth strategy had to be 100% organic search. I didn't want to rely on paid ads. I needed an architecture that would allow me to scale globally from day one while maintaining perfect Core Web Vitals.
Here is a breakdown of the technical SEO architecture I built using Next.js to support 20 languages and over 1,500 static routes.
To capture global search volume, the app needed to be accessible in 20 different languages (from English and French to Japanese, Hindi, and Arabic).
Dynamic Middleware: I set up Next.js middleware to handle dynamic routing for all 20 locales seamlessly.
100% SSG: Speed is a massive ranking factor. Every single localized route is statically generated (SSG). 
Localized Metadata: It’s not just the UI that translates. The Title, Description, and OpenGraph tags are dynamically generated for the specific locale on the server before the page is served to the client.
Managing a sitemap for a multi-language site with 14 distinct tools can get messy fast. I built an automated sitemap generator that outputs all 1,500+ localized URLs with strict rules:
Hreflang Integration: Alternates (hreflang) are embedded directly inside the sitemap.xml and the HTML head to ensure Google never penalizes for duplicate content across regions.
Strict Prioritization: The generator assigns intelligent priorities automatically (e.g., 1.0 for the Home page, 0.9 for Alternative pages, 0.8 for individual Tools).
Freshness Tags: Explicit lastmod and changefreq tags are applied to every entry to guide crawler budgets.
Google loves structured data. Instead of just adding a basic schema, I programmatically injected specific JSON-LD schemas across all 14 tool pages:
SoftwareApplication Schema: Applied globally across all tool pages to define exactly what the a

zh.title: 我如何用Next.js构建了一个拥有1,500+本地化路由和完美技术SEO的应用
zh.summary: ProMetadata使用Next.js在20种语言中提供1,500+静态生成的路由，实现动态中间件、hreflang标签和自动化站点地图生成。该架构在保持完美的Core Web Vitals的同时实现了大规模的多语言SEO。

fr.title: Comment j'ai construit une application Next.js avec 1 500+ routes localisées et un SEO technique parfait
fr.summary: ProMetadata utilise Next.js pour servir plus de 1 500 routes générées statiquement dans 20 langues, implémentant des middleware dynamiques, des balises hreflang et une génération automatique de sitemap. L'architecture réalise le SEO multilingue à grande échelle tout en maintenant une Core Web Vitals parfaite.

de.title: Wie ich eine Next.js-App mit über 1.500 lokalisierten Routen und perfektem Technical SEO gebaut habe
de.summary: ProMetadata nutzt Next.js, um über 1.500 statisch generierte Routen in 20 Sprachen bereitzustellen, mit dynamischen Middleware, hreflang-Tags und automatisierter Sitemap-Generierung. Die Architektur erreicht mehrsprachige SEO im großen Maßstab und behält dabei perfekte Core Web Vitals.

es.title: Cómo construí una aplicación Next.js con 1.500+ rutas localizadas y SEO técnico perfecto
es.summary: ProMetadata utiliza Next.js para servir más de 1.500 rutas generadas estáticamente en 20 idiomas, implementando middleware dinámico, etiquetas hreflang y generación automática de mapas del sitio. La arquitectura logra SEO multilingüe a escala mientras mantiene Web Vitals principales perfectas.

title: VoltAgent Just Asked Us to Build Their Guardrail Provider Interface. Here Is What We Shipped.
summary: A decoupled guardrail provider interface was designed for VoltAgent's AI agent framework, enabling third-party implementations to plug in without vendor lock-in. Providers evaluate input and output with pass/fail/modify decisions independent of the core framework.

zh.title: VoltAgent要求我们构建他们的护栏提供商接口。以下是我们交付的内容。
zh.summary: 为VoltAgent的AI代理框架设计了一个解耦的护栏提供商接口，使第三方实现无需供应商锁定即可集成。提供商以独立于核心框架的通过/失败/修改决策来评估输入和输出。

fr.title: VoltAgent nous a demandé de construire leur interface de fournisseur de garde-fous. Voici ce que nous avons livré.
fr.summary: Une interface de fournisseur de garde-fous découplée a été conçue pour le cadre d'agent IA de VoltAgent, permettant aux implémentations tierces de se brancher sans verrouillage du fournisseur. Les fournisseurs évaluent l'entrée et la sortie avec des décisions pass/fail/modify indépendantes du cadre principal.

de.title: VoltAgent forderte uns auf, ihre Schnittstelle für Schutzvorrichtungsanbieter zu bauen. Hier ist, was wir bereitgestellt haben.
de.summary: Eine entkoppelte Schutzvorrichtungsanbieter-Schnittstelle wurde für VoltAgents KI-Agent-Framework entwickelt, die es Third-Party-Implementierungen ermöglicht, ohne Herstellerbindung angeschlossen zu werden. Anbieter bewerten Ein- und Ausgaben mit Pass/Fail/Modify-Entscheidungen unabhängig vom Kernframework.

es.title: VoltAgent nos pidió que construyéramos su interfaz de proveedores de protecciones. Aquí es lo que entregamos.
es.summary: Se diseñó una interfaz de proveedor de protecciones desacoplada para el marco de agentes IA de VoltAgent, permitiendo que implementaciones de terceros se conecten sin bloqueo de proveedor. Los proveedores evalúan entrada y salida con decisiones de pasar/fallar/modificar independientes del marco principal.

title: Why CSPM Alone Won't Save Your Multi-Cloud Compliance in 2026
summary: Cloud Security Posture Management alone is insufficient for comprehensive multi-cloud compliance in 2026. Additional tools and approaches are needed to address the growing complexity of managing security and compliance across multiple cloud providers.
content: Running multi-cloud compliance for your startup? I'd love to hear what combination of tools is working (or not working) for your team. Drop a comment.

zh.title: 为什么仅CSPM无法在2026年保障您的多云合规性
zh.summary: 云安全态势管理单独不足以在2026年实现全面的多云合规。需要额外的工具和方法来应对跨多个云提供商管理安全性和合规性的日益复杂性。

fr.title: Pourquoi CSPM seul ne sauvera pas votre conformité multi-cloud en 2026
fr.summary: La gestion de la posture de sécurité du cloud à elle seule est insuffisante pour une conformité multi-cloud complète en 2026. Des outils et approches supplémentaires sont nécessaires pour résoudre la complexité croissante de la gestion de la sécurité et de la conformité sur plusieurs fournisseurs de cloud.

de.title: Warum CSPM allein Ihre Multi-Cloud-Compliance 2026 nicht retten wird
de.summary: Cloud Security Posture Management allein ist unzureichend für umfassende Multi-Cloud-Compliance 2026. Zusätzliche Tools und Ansätze sind erforderlich, um die wachsende Komplexität der Verwaltung von Sicherheit und Compliance über mehrere Cloud-Anbieter hinweg zu bewältigen.

es.title: Por qué CSPM solo no salvará su cumplimiento multi-nube en 2026
es.summary: La Gestión de Posturas de Seguridad en la Nube por sí sola es insuficiente para el cumplimiento multi-nube integral en 2026. Se necesitan herramientas y enfoques adicionales para abordar la creciente complejidad de administrar la seguridad y el cumplimiento en múltiples proveedores de nube.

title: 5 PDF Tools That Don't Upload Your Files (And Why That Matters)
summary: Five client-side PDF tools from Parchment—merge, split, compress, extract text, password protect—process files entirely in the browser using pdf-lib and canvas APIs, ensuring sensitive documents never reach a server.
content:
Most online PDF tools work the same way: you upload your file, their server processes it, and you download the result. Your document — contracts, tax forms, medical records — passes through someone else's infrastructure.
Here are 5 PDF tools that work differently. They run entirely in your browser. Your files never leave your device.
Combine multiple PDFs into a single document. Drag to reorder. No file size limits (your browser is the only constraint).
Why it matters: Merging contracts or financial documents on a third-party server means those documents exist on that server — even briefly.
🔗 Merge PDFs — Parchment
Extract specific pages or split a PDF into multiple files. Select pages visually or enter page ranges.
Why it matters: If you're extracting pages from an NDA or employee record, "briefly on a server" is still a liability.
🔗 Split PDF — Parchment
Reduce PDF file size for email or storage. Adjustable quality settings. Works on image-heavy PDFs.
Why it matters: Compression typically requires heavy server-side processing. Parchment does it client-side using canvas resampling.
🔗 Compress PDF — Parchment
Extract all text content from a PDF. Useful for searching, indexing, or feeding into other tools.
Why it matters: Text extraction means the tool reads every word of your document. Do you want a remote server doing that?
🔗 PDF to Text — Parchment
Add password protection to any PDF. Set permissions for printing, copying, and editing.
Why it matters: The irony of uploading a document to a server in order to password-protect it should not need explaining.
🔗 Protect PDF — Parchment
All five tools use pdf-lib and the browser's built-in canvas API. When you drop a file, JavaScript processes it in your browser tab. The file never touches a network request.
You can verify this yourself: open DevTools → Network tab → use any tool. Zero requests.
Parchment has 15 PDF tools — all free, all client-side, no signup:
Merge, Split, Compress, Rotate, Reorder
Images to PDF,

zh.title: 5个不上传文件的PDF工具（以及为什么这很重要）
zh.summary: Parchment推出的五种客户端PDF工具——合并、分割、压缩、提取文本、密码保护——使用pdf-lib和canvas API在浏览器中完全处理文件，确保敏感文档永远不会到达服务器。

fr.title: 5 outils PDF qui ne téléchargent pas vos fichiers (et pourquoi c'est important)
fr.summary: Cinq outils PDF côté client de Parchment—fusionner, diviser, compresser, extraire du texte, protéger par mot de passe—traitent les fichiers entièrement dans le navigateur en utilisant les API pdf-lib et canvas, garantissant que les documents sensibles ne atteignent jamais un serveur.

de.title: 5 PDF-Tools, die Ihre Dateien nicht hochladen (und warum das wichtig ist)
de.summary: Fünf Client-seitige PDF-Tools von Parchment – zusammenführen, teilen, komprimieren, Text extrahieren, Passwortschutz – verarbeiten Dateien vollständig im Browser unter Verwendung von pdf-lib- und Canvas-APIs, um sicherzustellen, dass sensible Dokumente niemals einen Server erreichen.

es.title: 5 herramientas PDF que no cargan sus archivos (y por qué importa)
es.summary: Cinco herramientas PDF del lado del cliente de Parchment—combinar, dividir, comprimir, extraer texto, proteger con contraseña—procesan archivos completamente en el navegador usando las APIs pdf-lib y canvas, asegurando que los documentos sensibles nunca lleguen a un servidor.
